content.text: The key file is likely `crates/pixi_spec/src/exclude_newer.rs`. Let me read that first
Now let me look at how the `exclude-newer` is resolved in the features_ext or workspace level

content.content:
1	use std::collections::HashSet;
2	
3	use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
4	use indexmap::{IndexMap, IndexSet};
5	use itertools::Itertools;
6	use miette::Diagnostic;
7	use pixi_spec::{ExcludeNewer, ResolvedExcludeNewer};
8	use pixi_spec_containers::DependencyMap;
9	use rattler_conda_types::{
10	    ChannelConfig, ChannelUrl, NamedChannelOrUrl, ParseChannelError, Platform,
11	};
12	
13	use crate::{
14	    CondaConstraints, CondaDependencies, PrioritizedChannel, PyPiDependencies, SpecType,
15	    SystemRequirements,
16	    dependencies::CondaDevDependencies,
17	    has_features_iter::HasFeaturesIter,
18	    has_manifest_ref::HasWorkspaceManifest,
19	    pypi::{ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer, pypi_options::PypiOptions},
20	    workspace::{ChannelPriority, SolveStrategy},
21	};
22	
23	/// ChannelPriorityCombination error, thrown when multiple channel priorities
24	/// are set
25	#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error, Diagnostic)]
26	#[error("Multiple channel priorities are not allowed in a single environment")]
27	pub struct ChannelPriorityCombinationError;
28	
29	/// A trait that implement various methods for collections that combine
30	/// attributes of Features It is implemented by Environment, GroupedEnvironment
31	/// and SolveGroup. Remove some of the boilerplate of combining features and its
32	/// derived data from multiple sources.
33	///
34	/// The name of the lifetime parameter is named `'source` that the borrow comes
35	/// from the source of the data for most implementations this will be the pixi
36	/// project.
37	///
38	/// There is blanket implementation available for all types that implement
39	/// [`HasWorkspaceManifest`] and [`HasFeaturesIter`]
40	pub trait FeaturesExt<'source>: HasWorkspaceManifest<'source> + HasFeaturesIter<'source> {
41	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection, preserving the
42	    /// effective metadata for the first winning entry of every unique channel.
43	    fn prioritized_channels(
44	        &self,
45	    ) -> IndexMap<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl, &'source PrioritizedChannel> {
46	        let channels = self.features().flat_map(|feature| match &feature.channels {
47	            Some(channels) => channels,
48	            None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.channels,
49	        });
50	
51	        let mut prioritized = IndexMap::new();
52	        for channel in channels.into_iter().sorted_by(|a, b| {
53	            let a = a.priority.unwrap_or(0);
54	            let b = b.priority.unwrap_or(0);
55	            b.cmp(&a)
56	        }) {
57	            prioritized.entry(&channel.channel).or_insert(channel);
58	        }
59	
60	        prioritized
61	    }
62	
63	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection.
64	    ///
65	    /// Users can specify custom channels on a per-feature basis. This method
66	    /// collects and deduplicates all the channels from all the features in
67	    /// the order they are defined in the manifest.
68	    ///
69	    /// If a feature does not specify any channel the default channels from the
70	    /// project metadata are used instead.
71	    fn channels(&self) -> IndexSet<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl> {
72	        self.prioritized_channels().into_keys().collect()
73	    }
74	
75	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection.
76	    ///
77	    /// This function is similar to [`Self::channels]` but it resolves the
78	    /// channel urls using the provided channel config.
79	    fn channel_urls(
80	        &self,
81	        channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
82	    ) -> Result<Vec<ChannelUrl>, ParseChannelError> {
83	        self.channels()
84	            .into_iter()
85	            .cloned()
86	            .map(|channel| channel.into_base_url(channel_config))
87	            .collect()
88	    }
89	
90	    /// Returns the channel priority, error on multiple, different values, return None if
91	    /// no value is set.
92	    ///
93	    /// When using multiple channel priorities over different features we should
94	    /// error as the user should decide what they want.
95	    fn channel_priority(&self) -> Result<Option<ChannelPriority>, ChannelPriorityCombinationError> {
96	        let mut channel_priority = None;
97	        for feature in self.features() {
98	            if let Some(priority) = feature.channel_priority {
99	                // If we already have a priority and it's different, error
100	                if channel_priority.is_some() && channel_priority != Some(priority) {
101	                    return Err(ChannelPriorityCombinationError);
102	                }
103	                channel_priority = Some(priority);
104	            }
105	        }
106	        Ok(channel_priority)
107	    }
108	
109	    /// Returns the raw workspace exclude-newer configuration before channel and
110	    /// package-specific overrides are applied.
111	    fn exclude_newer_raw(&self) -> Option<ExcludeNewer> {
112	        self.workspace_manifest().workspace.exclude_newer
113	    }
114	
115	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration.
116	    fn exclude_newer_config(
117	        &self,
118	    ) -> Result<Option<rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
119	        self.exclude_newer_config_resolved()
120	            .map(|exclude_newer| exclude_newer.map(Into::into))
121	    }
122	
123	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
124	    fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
125	        &self,
126	    ) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
127	        let mut exclude_newer = self
128	            .exclude_newer_raw()
129	            .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));
130	
131	        for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
132	            let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
133	                continue;
134	            };
135	
136	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
137	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
138	            });
139	
140	            *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
141	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
142	                    .clone()
143	                    .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
144	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
145	                    channel.channel.to_string(),
146	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
147	                ),
148	            };
149	        }
150	
151	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
152	            .workspace_manifest()
153	            .workspace
154	            .exclude_newer_package_overrides
155	        {
156	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
157	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
158	            });
159	
160	            *config = match package_exclude_newer {
161	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
162	                    config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
163	                }
164	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
165	                    .clone()
166	                    .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
167	            };
168	        }
169	
170	        Ok(exclude_newer)
171	    }
172	
173	    /// Returns the effective PyPI exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
174	    fn pypi_exclude_newer_config_resolved(&self) -> ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer {
175	        let mut exclude_newer = self
176	            .exclude_newer_raw()
177	            .map(|config| ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()))
178	            .unwrap_or_default();
179	
180	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
181	            .workspace_manifest()
182	            .workspace
183	            .pypi_exclude_newer_package_overrides
184	        {
185	            exclude_newer = match package_exclude_newer {
186	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
187	                    exclude_newer.with_package_cutoff(name.as_normalized().clone(), *dt)
188	                }
189	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => exclude_newer.with_package_cutoff(
190	                    name.as_normalized().clone(),
191	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff(),
192	                ),
193	            };
194	        }
195	
196	        exclude_newer
197	    }
198	
199	    /// Returns the strategy for solving packages.
200	    ///
201	    /// The chosen strategy is the first explicitly declared one in a feature
202	    /// as they are provided by the [`HasFeaturesIter::features`] iterator.
203	    ///
204	    /// If no feature declares a strategy, the default value of [`SolveStrategy`] is used.
205	    fn solve_strategy(&self) -> SolveStrategy {
206	        self.features()
207	            .flat_map(|feature| feature.solve_strategy)
208	            .next()
209	            .unwrap_or_default()
210	    }
211	
212	    /// Returns the platforms that this collection is compatible with.
213	    ///
214	    /// Which platforms a collection support depends on which platforms the
215	    /// selected features of the collection supports. The platforms that are
216	    /// supported by the collection is the intersection of the platforms
217	    /// supported by its features.
218	    ///
219	    /// Features can specify which platforms they support through the
220	    /// `platforms` key. If a feature does not specify any platforms the
221	    /// features defined by the project are used.
222	    fn platforms(&self) -> HashSet<Platform> {
223	        self.features()
224	            .map(|feature| {
225	                match &feature.platforms {
226	                    Some(platforms) => platforms,
227	                    None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.platforms,
228	                }
229	                .iter()
230	                .copied()
231	                .collect::<HashSet<_>>()
232	            })
233	            .reduce(|accumulated_platforms, feat| {
234	                accumulated_platforms.intersection(&feat).copied().collect()
235	            })
236	            .unwrap_or_default()
237	    }
238	
239	    /// Returns the system requirements for this collection.
240	    ///
241	    /// The system requirements of the collection are the union of the system
242	    /// requirements of all the features in the collection. If multiple
243	    /// features specify a requirement for the same system package, the
244	    /// highest is chosen.
245	    fn local_system_requirements(&self) -> SystemRequirements {
246	        self.features()
247	            .map(|feature| &feature.system_requirements)
248	            .fold(SystemRequirements::default(), |acc, req| {
249	                acc.union(req)
250	                    .expect("system requirements should have been validated upfront")
251	            })
252	    }
253	
254	    /// Returns true if any of the features has any reference to a pypi
255	    /// dependency.
256	    fn has_pypi_dependencies(&self) -> bool {
257	        self.features().any(|f| f.has_pypi_dependencies())
258	    }
259	
260	    /// Returns the PyPi dependencies to install for this collection.
261	    ///
262	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
263	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
264	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
265	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
266	    fn pypi_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> PyPiDependencies {
267	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
268	            .features()
269	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
270	            .filter_map(|f| f.pypi_dependencies(platform))
271	            .collect();
272	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
273	    }
274	
275	    /// Returns the dependencies to install for this collection.
276	    ///
277	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
278	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
279	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
280	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
281	    ///
282	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
283	    /// into consideration.
284	    fn dependencies(&self, kind: SpecType, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDependencies {
285	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
286	            .features()
287	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
288	            .filter_map(|f| f.dependencies(kind, platform))
289	            .collect();
290	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
291	    }
292	
293	    /// Returns the combined dependencies to install for this collection.
294	    ///
295	    /// The `build` dependencies overwrite the `host` dependencies which
296	    /// overwrite the `run` dependencies.
297	    ///
298	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
299	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
300	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
301	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
302	    ///
303	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
304	    /// into consideration.
305	    fn combined_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDependencies {
306	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
307	            .features()
308	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
309	            .filter_map(|f| f.combined_dependencies(platform))
310	            .collect();
311	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
312	    }
313	
314	    /// Returns the combined dev dependencies to install for this collection.
315	    ///
316	    /// Dev dependencies from all features in the group are collected and
317	    /// merged. If multiple features define the same dev dependency, the
318	    /// last one wins (later features override earlier ones).
319	    fn combined_dev_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDevDependencies {
320	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
321	            .features()
322	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
323	            .filter_map(|f| f.dev_dependencies(platform))
324	            .collect();
325	
326	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
327	    }
328	
329	    /// Returns the combined version constraints for this collection.
330	    ///
331	    /// Constraints from all features are combined. If multiple features define
332	    /// a constraint for the same package, all constraints are retained and the
333	    /// solver must satisfy all of them simultaneously.
334	    ///
335	    /// If the `platform` is `None`, no platform specific constraints are taken
336	    /// into consideration.
337	    fn combined_constraints(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaConstraints {
338	        let constraints: Vec<_> = self
339	            .features()
340	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
341	            .filter_map(|f| f.constraints(platform))
342	            .collect();
343	        DependencyMap::merge_all(constraints.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
344	    }
345	
346	    /// Returns the pypi options for this collection.
347	    ///
348	    /// The pypi options of all features are combined. They will be combined in
349	    /// the order that they are defined in the manifest.
350	    /// The index-url is a special case and can only be defined once. This
351	    /// should have been verified beforehand.
352	    fn pypi_options(&self) -> PypiOptions {
353	        // Collect all the pypi-options from the features in one set,
354	        // deduplicate them and sort them on feature index, default feature comes last.
355	        let pypi_options: Vec<_> = self
356	            .features()
357	            .filter_map(|feature| {
358	                if feature.pypi_options().is_none() {
359	                    self.workspace_manifest().workspace.pypi_options.as_ref()
360	                } else {
361	                    feature.pypi_options()
362	                }
363	            })
364	            .collect();
365	
366	        // Merge all the pypi options into one.
367	        pypi_options
368	            .into_iter()
369	            .fold(PypiOptions::default(), |acc, opts| {
370	                acc.union(opts)
371	                    .expect("merging of pypi-options should already have been checked")
372	            })
373	    }
374	}
375	
376	impl<'source, FeatureCollection> FeaturesExt<'source> for FeatureCollection where
377	    FeatureCollection: HasWorkspaceManifest<'source> + HasFeaturesIter<'source>
378	{
379	}
380

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Let me look at `exclude_newer_config_resolved` more carefully.

Looking at lines 124-171:

1. First, `exclude_newer_raw()` is called which gets `workspace.workspace.exclude_newer` - the workspace-level setting.
2. Then channel-level settings are applied as overrides.
3. Then package-level settings are applied.

The problem is the semantics. The bug description says: "It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]`"

Wait, let me re-read the bug description more carefully:

> The `workspace.exclude-newer` overrides the channel version, which I believe to be wrong.
> Expected behavior: It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO.

So the user expects that channel-level `exclude-newer` should take precedence over
